title: Reimburse Ikigai Info Governance Action Deposit.

rationale.summary: Vote: NO. For this TreasuryWithdrawals (active), downside risk and/or weak safeguards outweighed demonstrated benefit in the current evidence set. Why: Pinned anchor document is available for this action. Additional context: Treasury withdrawal actions require elevated scrutiny. Confidence context: score -0.63; confidence 100.0%.
rationale.summary_raw: DRep ratification support is material but below threshold; treated as a modest context signal.
top_fixes: Use stable, reproducible anchor links (commit-pinned when possible).
Add line-item budget with milestone-gated disbursement and objective completion checks.
Document downside risks, mitigations, and what gets cut first if delivery slips.

sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Treasury withdrawal to reimburse the unrecovered deposit from the Ikigai Info governance action and compensate claimed lost staking rewards.
Recipient: Ikigai Info governance action submitter; specific recipient address not stated in document
Deliverables: Instant on-chain distribution of the withdrawal amount to the intended recipient as part of governance action enactment

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The proposal says an Info governance action titled "Cardanoの生きがい - Ikigai -" was submitted in September 2024 shortly after the Chang hard fork.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, low materiality): The proposal says the Ikigai action was symbolic and asked the community to agree with a statement thanking contributors and expressing hope for Cardano's future.

Claim (technical, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The proposal says a Cardano node bug allowed an unregistered stake key to be used in the governance action, preventing the submitter from recovering a 100000 ADA deposit.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The proposal says community sentiment at the time favored reimbursing the deposit because the submitter should not lose 100000 ADA for being an early governance pioneer.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The proposal says no entity-based budget submissions included this reimbursement after treasury withdrawals became available following the Plomin hard fork.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The proposal says the Cardano in Oceania initiative included the reimbursement in its budget Info governance action, but that action was not approved.
missing: Independent evidence for: The proposal says an Info governance action titled "Cardanoの生きがい - Ikigai -" was submitted in September 2024 shortly after the Chang hard fork.
Independent evidence for: The proposal says community sentiment at the time favored reimbursing the deposit because the submitter should not lose 100000 ADA for being an early governance pioneer.
Independent evidence for: The proposal says no entity-based budget submissions included this reimbursement after treasury withdrawals became available following the Plomin hard fork.
Independent evidence for: The proposal says the Cardano in Oceania initiative included the reimbursement in its budget Info governance action, but that action was not approved.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal says a Cardano node bug allowed an unregistered stake key to be used in the governance action, preventing the submitter from recovering a 100000 ADA deposit." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The proposal says an Info governance action titled "Cardanoの生きがい - Ikigai -" was submitted in September 2024 shortly after the Chang hard fork." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
